title: HUD Transparency Act of 2025
stage_label: Received in the Senate and Read twice and referred to the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs.
subjects: Department of Housing and Urban Development
Government ethics and transparency, public corruption
crs_summary: HUD Transparency Act of 2025 This bill requires the Office of Inspector General of the Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) to annually testify before Congress. Specifically, the office must testify regarding efforts to detect and prevent fraud, waste, and abuse; ability to conduct and supervise audits, investigations, and reviews; actions to identify opportunities for HUD programs to progress and succeed; recommendations to improve overall efficiency and public accountability; the extent to which HUD has resources sufficient to carry out its statutory mission; and  ongoing activities regarding any such additional work.
last_action_text: Received in the Senate and Read twice and referred to the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs.
